# TabForge AI Starter — LLM Context File
# This starter is a pre-configured Jakarta EE application with TabForge AI already set up.
# The developer cloned this starter and wants to write business logic on top of it.
# Full API reference: https://github.com/tabforgeai/tabforge-ai/blob/main/llms.txt

## What is already configured in this starter

- TabForge AI dependency (io.github.tabforgeai:tabforge-ai:1.1.0) in pom.xml
- `-parameters` compiler flag in pom.xml (required for EasyAI tool introspection)
- DynTabs multi-tab PrimeFaces UI — CDI, JSF navigation, tab shell template
- `src/main/resources/easyai.properties` — AI provider config (fill in your API key)
- Logback logging
- Jakarta EE 11 project structure (CDI, EJB, JSF ready)

The developer does NOT need to add any Maven dependencies or compiler flags.
They just need to write their tab beans and AI code.

---

## Configure AI (one-time)

Edit `src/main/resources/easyai.properties`:
```properties
easyai.provider=openai
easyai.api-key=sk-YOUR-KEY
easyai.model-name=gpt-4o-mini
```

For local Ollama (free, no key needed):
```properties
easyai.provider=ollama
easyai.model-name=llama3
```

---

## Add a DynTabs tab (typical task)

### 1. Create the tab bean
```java
@Named
@TabScoped
@DynTab(name = "OrdersDynTab", title = "Orders", includePage = "/WEB-INF/include/orders/orders.xhtml")
public class OrdersBean extends BaseDyntabCdiBean {

    @Inject OrderService orderService;
    private List<Order> orders;

    @Override
    protected void accessPointMethod(Map parameters) {
        orders = orderService.findAll();   // called when tab opens
    }

    @Override
    protected void exitPointMethod() {
        // cleanup when tab closes (optional)
    }

    public List<Order> getOrders() { return orders; }
}
```

### 2. Create the XHTML page
Place at: `src/main/webapp/WEB-INF/include/orders/orders.xhtml`

### 3. Add to the menu
```xml
<p:menuitem value="Orders" action="uishell:Orders"/>
```

That's it. DynTabs opens the tab, creates an isolated `@TabScoped` bean instance, calls `accessPointMethod()`.

### Key DynTabs patterns
```java
// Send message to all open tabs
sendMessageToAllAppModules(myPayload);

// Receive message in a tab
@Override
public void onApplicationMessage(Object message) { ... }

// Child tab returns value to parent and closes
closeAndReturnValueToCaller(selectedValue);

// Receive return value in parent tab
@Override
public void onReturnedValue(Object value) { ... }

// Open a tab programmatically with parameters
// (from a menu action or button)
```

---

## Add AI features

### Simple chat
```java
Conversation chat = EasyAI.chat()
    .withMemory(20)
    .withSystemMessage("You are a helpful assistant.")
    .build();

String answer = chat.send(userInput);
```

### AI assistant with your services (no @Tool annotations needed)
```java
@EasyAIAssistant(systemMessage = "You are an e-commerce support bot.")
public interface SupportBot {
    String ask(String question);
}

@Inject OrderService orderService;  // your existing EJB

SupportBot bot = EasyAI.assistant(SupportBot.class)
    .withTools(orderService)
    .build();

String answer = bot.ask("Where is order #12345?");
// AI calls orderService.findOrder("12345") automatically
```

### @Inject assistant (CDI — no build() needed)
```java
@EasyAIAssistant(
    systemMessage = "You are a support bot",
    tools = {OrderService.class}
)
public interface SupportBot {
    String ask(String question);
}

@Inject SupportBot bot;  // ready to use
```

### AI from documents (RAG)
```java
@EasyRAG(source = "classpath:company-policy.pdf")
@EasyAIAssistant(systemMessage = "Answer based on company policy.")
public interface PolicyBot {
    String ask(String question);
}

PolicyBot bot = EasyAI.assistant(PolicyBot.class).build();
```

### Autonomous multi-step agent
```java
// In a @Named @ApplicationScoped bean — build in @PostConstruct!
@PostConstruct
public void init() {
    agent = EasyAI.agent()
        .withServices(orderService, paymentService, shippingService)
        .withMaxSteps(10)
        .withPlanningPrompt(true)
        .withStepListener(step ->
            log.info("Step {}: {} -> {}", step.stepNumber(), step.toolName(), step.result()))
        .build();
}

String result = agent.execute("Process order #42: verify stock, charge card, schedule delivery");
```

---

## Error handling (always wrap AI calls)
```java
try {
    return bot.ask(userQuestion);
} catch (Exception e) {
    log.error("AI call failed", e);
    return "Sorry, something went wrong: " + EasyAI.extractErrorMessage(e);
}
```

---

## Common mistakes to avoid
- **Do NOT build EasyAgent at field initialization** in a CDI bean — `@Inject` fields are null at that point. Always use `@PostConstruct`.
- **Do NOT add quotes** to values in easyai.properties: `model-name=gpt-4o` not `model-name="gpt-4o"`.
- **Tool methods should return String** — or ensure `toString()` is meaningful.

---

## Full API reference
https://github.com/tabforgeai/tabforge-ai/blob/main/llms.txt
